name: Barney Kessel
abstract: Barney Kessel (October 17, 1923 – May 6, 2004) was an American jazz guitarist. Known in particular for his knowledge of chords and inversions and chord-based melodies, he was a member of many prominent jazz groups as well as a "first call" guitarist for studio, film, and television recording sessions.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Three Guitars with Charlie Byrd, Herb Ellis, and Barney Kessel
Charlie Byrd, Herb Ellis, and Barney Kessel: Three Guitars
Kool Jazz Festival: A Salute to Django Reinhardt

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: I think the first jazz guitar I really heard was Barney Kessel. And that sold me on the idea that I wanted to play jazz on the guitar and not rock & roll.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: We went on tour in 1967... I was part of the guitar trio – Barney Kessel, Jim Hall, and myself
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Jolly
about_quotes.evidence: So I got to work with Jack Teagarden, Georgie Auld, Chet Baker, Herb Geller, Barney Kessel... Georgie Auld and Barney Kessel called me and said, “We have a gig out here in the valley in Studio City, do you want to come over on the fourth of July and work?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Jolly interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Cal Collins
about_quotes.evidence: myself included, are really influenced by the straight ahead, mainstream swing guitar, Barney Kessel type things
about_quotes.doc_title: Cal Collins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: The Gramercy Five, with Dodo [Marmarosa] and Roy [Eldridge], they were pretty modern. Barney Kessel and Roy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Pat Boone as having a band that has Frank Capp, Barney Kessel and Ray Brown and me for a rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999
